title: Dactyladenia gilletii
taxonomy: Dactyladenia gilletii is classified in the family Salicaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as willows. This plant is further grouped into the order Malpighiales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants. Within the order Malpighiales, Dactyladenia gilletii is placed in the suborder Malpighianae. The suborder Malpighianae is a subgroup of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Dactyladenia gilletii's classification is based on its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Dactyladenia gilletii has been found to have various medicinal properties in traditional African medicine. Its bark and roots are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is also used as a treatment for digestive issues and to reduce inflammation. In some regions, it is applied topically to wounds and cuts to promote healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its medicinal uses.

parts: Dactyladenia gilletii is a plant species that has been found to have various medicinal properties.
